items.headline: Dodgers’ Tyler Glasnow exits early against Astros with low back pain
items.description:
HOUSTON (AP) — Los Angeles Dodgers starter Tyler Glasnow left Tuesday game against the Houston Astros before the second inning because of low back pain.
Glasnow allowed a home run to leadoff hitter Brice Matthews before retiring the next three batters, with two strikeouts to end the first. He returned to the mound to warm up before the second inning but appeared to be in discomfort after throwing a pitch and signaled to the dugout.

items.headline: A relaxed Rory McIlroy says he's more motivated than ever after Masters repeat
items.description:
CHARLOTTE, N.C. (AP) — Rory McIlroy appeared rested and relaxed as he prepared for his first tournament since repeating as Masters champion.
Just don't let that be confused with complacency.

items.headline: Trailing 2-0 in series, Flyers hope return home can fuel comeback against Hurricanes
items.description:
PHILADELPHIA (AP) — The Philadelphia Flyers turned tenuous postseason hopes into a fashion statement when they wore T-shirts with “3.8%” on the sleeve to start their playoff run — a nod to their chances in March, per an NHL analytics and prediction platform.
Might be time for a new addition — courtesy of updated math — to their wardrobe.

items.headline: Joel Embiid is ruled out with ankle, hip injuries for the 76ers in Game 2 of their playoff series against the Knicks
items.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Joel Embiid is ruled out with ankle, hip injuries for the 76ers in Game 2 of their playoff series against the Knicks.

items.headline: Joel Embiid is out for Game 2 of the 76ers' series against the Knicks with ankle and hip injuries
items.description:
NEW YORK (AP) — Joel Embiid will miss Game 2 of the Philadelphia 76ers ' second-round series against the New York Knicks on Wednesday night with a sprained right ankle and a sore right hip.
The 76ers had listed their center as probable to play, mentioning only the ankle, before adding the hip problem and ruling him out on the injury report about six hours before trying to even the Eastern Conference semifinals.
